❯ Run exactly this in bash and wait for it, then reply DONE: sleep 35; echo
  slept

● Bash(sleep 35; echo slept)
  ⎿  Error: Blocked: sleep 35 followed by: echo slept. To wait for a condition,
     use Monitor with an until-loop (e.g. `until <check>; do sleep 2; done`). To
     wait for a command you started, use run_in_background: true. Do not chain
     shorter sleeps to work around this block.

✢ Boondoggling… (3s · ↑ 133 tokens)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
❯ 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle) · esc to interrupt
